addressed: GHSA-vxr8-fq34-vvx9: Trusted Types policy survives clearConfig() and can poison later RETURN_TRUSTED_TYPE output
GHSA-gvmj-g25r-r7wr: SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES bypass - template expressions survive sanitization inside <template> content
GHSA-cmwh-pvxp-8882: Permanent ALLOWED_ATTR pollution via setConfig() bypassing the hook clone-guard
structured_output.reasoning: The lockfile pinned a hoisted node_modules/dompurify at version 3.4.7 (resolved from jspdf's optional dependency ^3.3.1), which was affected by three moderate-severity HTML-sanitization bypass advisories fixed in 3.4.11. The two direct DOMPurify consumers (superset-ui-core and legacy-preset-chart-nvd3) already declared ^3.4.11 and had nested 3.4.11 copies, but the hoisted copy used by jspdf remained at 3.4.7. Running `npm update dompurify --package-lock-only` bumped the hoisted copy to 3.4.11 (which still satisfies jspdf's ^3.3.1 constraint) and deduplicated the nested copies. Post-fix npm audit confirms no dompurify advisories remain.
